_notes_on_removed_patterns.fact_forcing_gate: REMOVED v12.1.5 after live-smoke forensics (workspace bridge-t7-G3mPzn). The ECC gateguard PreToolUse hook fires once per Write attempt and Claude recovers natively — it presents the 4 requested facts and retries the Write. Neither extend_timeout nor auto_respond is appropriate: extend_timeout doesn't help (Claude isn't slow, it's just sequencing tool calls), and auto_respond stacks identical messages at the prompt every poll-tick because the gate error remains visible in pane scrollback, interfering with Claude's own recovery. The correct bridge behavior is no rule — let Claude handle the hook natively. Re-introduce only with dedupe logic that suppresses re-fires within a single agent turn (v12.1.6+ design).
